title: Fix remaining 'orchestrator' references in CLI output and onboarding
description:
After the project rename to 'agent-takt', several user-visible strings still say 'orchestrator' instead of 'takt'. Identified from running 'takt init':

1. Header banner: '=== orchestrator init ===' → '=== takt init ==='
2. Final message: 'Run `orchestrator summary` to verify the setup.' → 'Run `takt summary` to verify the setup.'
3. Gitignore message: 'Updated .gitignore with orchestrator entries' → 'Updated .gitignore with takt entries'

Search the full codebase (src/agent_takt/) for any remaining user-visible string literals containing 'orchestrator' and replace them with 'takt'. This includes console output, error messages, log lines, and any help text. Do NOT rename identifiers, class names, variable names, or internal symbols — only user-facing string content.

Files likely involved: src/agent_takt/onboarding.py, src/agent_takt/cli.py. Check all files under src/agent_takt/ for completeness.

Acceptance criteria:
- 'takt init' output contains no references to 'orchestrator'.
- Grep for user-visible string literals containing 'orchestrator' in src/agent_takt/ returns no results (excluding internal identifiers/comments).
- All existing tests pass.

handoff_summary.completed: All user-facing string literals containing 'orchestrator' replaced with 'takt': banner header (=== takt init ===), final message (Run `takt summary`), gitignore comment (# takt) and log message (Updated .gitignore with takt entries), config file comment/header (takt configuration / takt init / takt run), merge retry instructions (takt merge), argparse prog name (takt), TUI help text and status message, error message for missing runner binary, scheduler auto-commit message prefix ([takt]).
handoff_summary.risks: Low. Changes are purely string literal replacements in user-facing output; no logic paths altered. The argparse prog change (cli.py:137) affects usage/help text displayed by --help.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should verify takt init output contains no 'orchestrator' references and existing tests pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. All changes are confined to string literals with no overlap with other in-progress beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Only user-facing string content was changed — output written to stdout/stderr, help text, error messages, git commit messages, and generated file content (config.yaml header, .gitignore comment). Internal identifiers (orchestrator_state_dir parameter, core/base-orchestrator skill name strings), docstrings, and the internal AI prompt description in prompts.py were intentionally left unchanged per the bead spec which explicitly prohibits renaming internal symbols.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should: (1) run `takt init` (or capture its output) and verify no 'orchestrator' appears; (2) grep src/agent_takt/ for user-visible 'orchestrator' string literals to confirm none remain; (3) verify `takt --help` shows prog='takt'; (4) run full test suite to check no regressions from string changes.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified. The few remaining 'orchestrator' occurrences in the codebase are all internal identifiers or docstrings, which are explicitly out of scope per the bead description.

conflict_risks: None. All changes are confined to string literals with no overlap with other in-progress beads.

execution_history.summary: Replaced all user-visible 'orchestrator' string literals with 'takt' across four files. No identifiers, class names, variable names, or internal symbols were modified.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
